title: Apply Clone View and Enhanced Editing UX to Dictionary Tab
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the clone view and enhanced editing UX from the ERD page available on the Dictionary and Migration tabs, so that I can duplicate and edit views consistently across all areas of the application.
description:
The clone view feature and enhanced editing UX that were built for the ERD page need to be applied to the Dictionary and Migration tabs. This ensures a consistent experience when managing Object Views regardless of which tab the user is on.

The clone functionality allows users to duplicate an existing view as a starting point for a new one. The enhanced editing UX includes the non-destructive exploration mode and explicit save workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Clone view button is available on the Dictionary tab's views panel
Clone view button is available on the Migration tab's views panel
Cloning creates a copy of the selected view with a new name
Enhanced editing UX (non-destructive exploration, explicit save) works on Dictionary and Migration tabs
Behavior matches the ERD page implementation
ux_changes: Adds clone view button and enhanced editing UX (non-destructive exploration, explicit save workflow) to the Dictionary and Migration tabs, matching the ERD page behavior.
middleware_changes: Reuses the existing clone view server-side logic. The clone endpoint is called from the Dictionary/Migration context the same way it is from ERD.
implementation_notes: Developer noted the Migration tab also needed the same update and applied it to both. Used a feature branch workflow: created branch from development, implemented, tested locally, merged to development, then to staging.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed Migration tab also needed the update and applied clone view to both Dictionary and Migration. Used a standard branching workflow with testing on development and staging environments.
